questions.question: The Forest Owlet was recently spotted in which National Park of Madhya Pradesh after 113 years?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Forest Owlet was spotted in Kuno National Park after 113 years. It belongs to the owl family Strigidae. It is endemic to central India and found in Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra and Gujarat, including Melghat Tiger Reserve. It lives in tropical and subtropical moist lowland forests, dense and open dry deciduous teak forests. It has an unspotted crown, full throat collar, thickly feathered legs, and heavily banded wings and tail. It is diurnal and hunts during the day.

questions.question: The 30 kW Wide Band Gap (WBG)-based Integrated Drive System was launched by which ministry?

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways

options.label: D
options.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

answer.label: D
answer.text: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology

questions.explanation: An indigenously developed 30 kiloWatt Wide Band Gap based Integrated Drive System for Electric Vehicles was launched by Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology in Chennai. It was developed by Centre for Development of Advanced Computing Thiruvananthapuram with Indian Institute of Technology Madras and Lucas TVS under National Mission on Power Electronics Technology. It was launched by the Secretary of Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology at IIT Madras. The 30 kiloWatt system suits electric passenger vehicles like compact cars and fleet mobility. It reduces import dependence on semiconductor-based Electric Vehicle powertrain systems.

questions.question: Which state has become the first country in the United Kingdom to allow water cremation, also called hydrolysis?
questions.explanation: Scotland became the first country in the United Kingdom to allow water cremation, also called hydrolysis. The new rules were approved by the Scottish Parliament, marking the biggest funeral law reform since 1902. Hydrolysis uses water and potassium hydroxide at about 150 degree Celsius to break down soft tissues, leaving only bones, which are dried and turned into ashes. In 2023 public consultation, 84 percent supported hydrolysis. The method is already legal in 28 United States states and countries like Canada, Ireland, Australia and New Zealand.

questions.question: Which country has become the first in the Americas to be officially verified for the elimination of leprosy?
questions.explanation: The World Health Organization (WHO) and the Pan American Health Organization (PAHO) have officially verified that Chile has eliminated Leprosy, also known as Hansen’s disease. With this achievement, Chile has become the first country in the Americas and the second country globally to be officially verified as having eliminated the disease. Leprosy was first recorded in Chile in the late 19th century, primarily on Rapa Nui (also known as Easter Island). The disease remained largely confined to the island, while mainland Chile experienced only occasional sporadic cases.

questions.question: Which state government has launched the MeHEALTH app for getting information about the nearby government health facilities?
questions.explanation: The Kerala Health Department has launched the MeHEALTH mobile application to help citizens access information about nearby government healthcare facilities. The app enables users to locate government hospitals within a 10-kilometre radius, making it easier for patients to identify accessible medical services. Through the platform, users can view detailed information about hospitals. A key feature of the app is the facility to book doctor appointments online, allowing patients to schedule consultations in advance. MeHEALTH app aims to improve accessibility, transparency, and convenience in public healthcare services in Kerala.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Tamil Nadu government in March 2026 to encourage women entrepreneurship?

options.label: A
options.text: Tamil Nadu Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Scheme

answer.label: A
answer.text: Tamil Nadu Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Scheme

questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin launched the Tamil Nadu Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Scheme (TWEES) to support one lakh women in starting businesses over five years. The scheme is designed by the Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSME) Department to promote women’s entrepreneurship across the State. The scheme aims to provide training, resources, and opportunities to encourage women-led enterprises.

questions.question: The Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) is developed by which country?
questions.explanation: The Precision Strike Missile (PrSM) was recently used by the United States for the first time in operations by United States Central Command during the conflict with Iran. It is a surface-to-surface guided ballistic missile designed to strike long-distance targets with very high precision. The missile is developed mainly for the United States Army by Lockheed Martin, a major US aerospace and defence company. It has an open system architecture and modular design, allowing upgrades and integration with new technologies.

questions.question: What is the IUCN status of ‘Sambar deer’ that was recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: The Wildlife Wing of the Himachal Pradesh Forest Department recently recorded sambar deer in high-altitude protected areas of Chamba district using camera traps for the first time. Sambar deer is a large deer species native to the Indian subcontinent, southern China and Southeast Asia. It is naturally found in India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Myanmar, the Philippines, China, Taiwan, Borneo, Malaysia, Sumatra and Java. The species lives mainly in forested hills, thick forests, swamp forests and open scrublands, often near cultivated areas. According to the International Union for Conservation of Nature Red List, its conservation status is Vulnerable.

questions.question: Human Metapneumovirus (HMPV) has recently spread rapidly across the West Coast of which country?
questions.explanation: Human Metapneumovirus (HMPV) is a highly contagious respiratory virus spreading rapidly on the West Coast of the United States (US). It belongs to the Pneumoviridae family, which also includes Respiratory Syncytial Virus (RSV). It mainly causes common cold–like respiratory illness. The virus spreads through air via coughing and sneezing, close personal contact, and touching contaminated surfaces then touching face. Common symptoms include cough, fever, nasal congestion, and shortness of breath. In severe cases, it may lead to bronchitis or pneumonia. No vaccine or specific antiviral treatment; care focuses on symptom management and preventing complications.

questions.question: The Proba-3 Mission is launched by which space agency?
questions.explanation: Proba-3 recently made news after European Space Agency lost contact with one of its two spacecraft. It is European Space Agency’s first mission focused on precision formation flying, where two satellites operate in extremely accurate coordination. The mission aims to create an artificial solar eclipse to study the Sun’s outer atmosphere (corona). This helps scientists observe the faint solar corona, which is normally hidden by the Sun’s intense brightness. The mission includes two small satellites: a Coronagraph spacecraft and an Occulter spacecraft shaped like the solar disc.
